nodes.label: Lorde
nodes.description: The main character. NZ singer-songwriter who somehow convinced the world Melodrama was about a house party. Emotionally broken in the most productive way possible.
details: "recently participated in a birth" as one does
Her mother proofread her 40,000 word master's thesis

nodes.label: Jack Antonoff
nodes.description: Producer, songwriter, general fuckboy from New Jersey. 13 years older than Lorde. Co-wrote and produced Melodrama while living with his girlfriend of 5 years. Nothing sus.
details: From: New Jersey (fuckboy capital of the world)
Grammy nominations: 3 wins out of 9 nominations
In 2014 said he was "desperate" for kids
SEO almost as bad as SUR's

nodes.label: Lena Dunham
nodes.description: Jack's girlfriend of 5 years, creator of Girls, worked on Hillary Clinton's campaign for 18 months. Was technically Lorde's landlord while Melodrama was being written in her apartment. Did not know about the album.
details: Worked on Hillary Clinton campaign for 18 months
Was friends with Lorde before meeting Jack
Let Lorde live in her apartment while recording
Left Lorde the apartment while she campaigned
Publicly broke up with Jack: December 2017

nodes.label: James Lowe
nodes.description: Lorde's ex-boyfriend. NZ photographer. Was 24 when Lorde was 16, which is a choice. Broke up in 2015. The official cover story for what Melodrama is about.
details: Met Lorde when she was ~16, he was ~24
New girlfriend: Sonja (not the mom, a different Sonja)

nodes.label: Taylor Swift
nodes.description: "Minor player. No, that's Becky." Was friends with Lena and Lorde separately before any of this. Jack co-wrote 4 songs on 1989. Taylor only has room for one snake in her home and it's herself.
details: Friends with Lorde: formerly, now estranged
Did embarrassing things like puffy paint jean jacket music videos
Conclusion: Taylor only has room for one snake in her home
Introduced Lorde and Jack at Grammy party (one account)

nodes.label: Sonja Yelich
nodes.description: Lorde's mom. Cool Mom. Proofread her daughter's 40,000 word master's thesis. Was cool with Lorde dating a 24-year-old at 16. She spells it with a sexy unique J.
details: Parenting style: extremely permissive re: age gaps

nodes.label: Grimes
nodes.description: One account says Lorde and Jack first met at a Grimes show in 2014, through Lena. Lorde and Lena were 'talking online' before any of this. A show through which pineapple juice was offered.
details: Role: possible meeting point for Lorde and Jack
Connection: Lena brought Jack to her show
Lorde: attended her show with Lena in 2014
The pineapple juice origin story begins here

nodes.label: Melodrama
nodes.description: Lorde's second album. Officially: a concept album about a house party exploring solitude after a breakup. Actually: a detailed diary of falling for your producer while living in his apartment with his girlfriend. Released June 2017.
details: Location: Jack Antonoff's home studio, Brooklyn
Official story: breakup album / house party concept
Real story: you are reading it

nodes.label: Pure Heroine
nodes.description: Lorde's debut album, released 2013. The one where she was a teenager writing smart suburban anthems. Before all of this happened.
Taylor Swift's album on which Jack co-wrote 4 songs. This is how Jack ended up in Lorde's orbit — Taylor introduced them at a Grammy party. Or they met at a Grimes show. Conflicting reports.
details: Relevance: Jack's connection to Taylor's squad
Led to: Lorde meeting Jack (one possible account)

nodes.label: The Bleachers
nodes.description: Jack Antonoff's band. Associated acts per Wikipedia screenshot include: Lorde, Carly Rae Jepsen, St. Vincent, Grimes, Chvrches. He gets around.
details: Associated acts: Lorde, Carly Rae, St. Vincent, Grimes

nodes.label: Green Light
nodes.description: The "first chapter" of the 2015-2017 era. Inspired at a Florence + the Machine concert Lorde attended with Jack. Took 18 months to write. The song where she's waiting to get her green light. Lena does not get a pineapple juice in this metaphor.
details: Inspired by: Florence + the Machine concert (with Jack)
Officially: about moving on from James
Piano line inspired by: Florence show lights

nodes.label: Sober
nodes.description: "Inspired by lord & jack's coachella trip??? Like LOL". The one about being King and Queen of the weekend when sober isn't an option. "But my hips have missed your hips." Presented without comment.
details: Suspected inspiration: Lorde & Jack's Coachella trip
Key lyric: "But my hips have missed your hips"
Key lyric: "We pretend that we just don't care"

nodes.label: The Louvre
nodes.description: "They'll hang us in the Louvre / down the back, but who cares – still the Louvre." A song about obsessive, world-consuming love. The "summer" is a recurring symbol — the summer Lena was gone, the summer that happened. "We banged all summer." Sub-text, allegedly.
details: Key lyric: "They'll hang us in the Louvre"
Key lyric: "We banged all summer" (yes really)
Note: Lorde & Taylor no longer friends. Lorde & Lena no longer friends. Do the math.

nodes.label: Liability
nodes.description: "Baby really hurt me, crying to the bar." The devastating one. "The truth is I am a toy that people enjoy / till all of the tricks don't work anymore." Lena comes back from the summer campaigning, this has gotten too big, Jack gets cold feet and possessive.
details: Key lyric: "Baby really hurt me, crying to the bar"
Key lyric: "I know that it's exciting running through the night"
Key lyric: "Jack and Jill got fucked up and possessive"
Context: Lena returns, things get complicated

nodes.label: Hard Feelings / Loveless
nodes.description: The speed-dating-producers quote comes from this era. "I was basically speed-dating different producers and songwriters in LA and hating it. And then I walked into a room with Jack Antonoff and just felt like home." Hard feelings, obviously.
details: Quote: "speed-dating different producers... walked into a room with Jack and felt like home"
Key lyric: "Bet you wanna rip my heart out"
Key lyric: "Gonna wanna tape my mouth shut"
Suspected: end of the Lorde/Jack situation
And then we get loveless, which is... a lot

nodes.label: Writer in the Dark
nodes.description: Written while sleeping next to someone while Lena slept. "I woke up in the middle of the night and was lying next to someone. And I wrote it down on my phone and I was like, 'Oh God, I feel so naughty writing this.' While somebody's sleeping, like an evil witch." Summer 16 is over, Lena done campaigning, Lorde trying to accept Jack isn't leaving Lena.
details: Written: in secret, next to someone, while Lena slept
Key lyric: "Bet you rue the day you kissed a writer in the dark"
Key lyric: "Now she's gonna play and sing and lock you in her heart"
Key lyric: "I'll find a way to be without you, babe"
Context: summer 16 ending, Lena back, Jack not leaving

nodes.label: Sober II (Melodrama)
nodes.description: Written in April 2016 — Sober and this song came around the same time. Lorde had "conflicted feelings" about it. The album-within-the-album that gives Melodrama its name. "I miss Melodrama. I miss you too."
details: Context: Lena back from 18-month campaign, things changing
Lorde: had "conflicted feelings" about the song

nodes.label: Grammy Party 2014
nodes.description: One account: Taylor Swift introduced Lorde and Jack at a Grammy after-party in 2014. Taylor got me a can of pineapple juice, she recalled. Jack struck her as appealingly intense. Another account says it was a Grimes show. Conflicting reports.
details: One account: Taylor introduced them here
Result: Jack gave her pineapple juice
Competing account: Grimes show (see that node)

nodes.label: Coachella 2016
nodes.description: Lorde and Jack went to Coachella together. This is when Lorde had her epiphany about what Melodrama was supposed to be — centred around Sober & Sober II. Sober is "inspired by lord & jack's coachella trip???" according to the author.

nodes.label: SNL March 2017
nodes.description: Lorde performed Green Light and Liability on SNL wearing a wedding dress, with Jack accompanying on piano — leaning back to back. "I wasn't fucking joking about the wedding dress on SNL. I will never get over this." Neither will we.

edges.label: collaborated / 💀
edges.description: Co-wrote and produced Melodrama together over 18 months. The collaboration that spawned an entire album's worth of evidence. "We were just obsessed with each other." Jack was 34. Lorde was 21. They recorded in the apartment he shared with his girlfriend.
details: Location: Jack's home studio in Brooklyn (his and Lena's apartment)
Quote: "We were just obsessed with each other"
Quote: "I walked into a room with Jack and just felt like home"
Plausible deniability: officially creative partnership only

edges.label: dated 5 years
edges.description: Jack and Lena dated for 5 years and lived together in Brooklyn. Lorde recorded Melodrama in their apartment. They publicly broke up in December 2017 — six months after the album dropped.
details: Breakup: December 2017, "slowly breaking up for 6 months"
Lena quote: watching Jack at Lorde's Brooklyn show

edges.label: friends / roommate
edges.description: Lorde and Lena were friends before either of them knew Jack — they'd been "talking online." Lorde moved into their Brooklyn apartment for extended recording sessions. Lena "lent emotional, if not material, sustenance." Their friendship did not survive whatever this was.
details: Lorde lived: in their apartment during recording
Lena's role: emotional support, food, sustenance

edges.label: ex (2013–2015)
edges.description: Lorde's long-term boyfriend before all of this. They met when she was ~16 and he was ~24 (a choice). Broke up in 2015. The official press narrative is that Melodrama is his breakup album. Reader, it is not his breakup album.
details: Green Light: officially about this breakup
Journalist take: supported the breakup narrative
Actual take: you are reading it

edges.label: former friends
edges.description: Were friends. Now are not. Lorde distanced herself from Taylor's squad while promoting Melodrama. Possible reason: loyalty to Lena, who Taylor is still friends with. Taylor only has room for one snake in her home and it's herself.
details: Lorde said: she was no longer part of the squad
Lorde & Lena: also no longer friends
Taylor covered: 2 Melodrama songs (for album promo)

edges.label: still friends
edges.description: Lena and Taylor are still friends as of the publication of this slideshow. Lorde is friends with neither of them anymore. Taylor only has room for one snake.
details: Lena was listed: in Taylor's 'Look What You Made Me Do' video pals

edges.label: co-wrote 1989
edges.description: Jack co-wrote 4 songs on Taylor Swift's 1989. This is how he ended up in Lorde's orbit — one account says Taylor introduced them at a Grammy party in 2014.
details: Result: entry into Taylor's friend group

edges.label: leads
edges.description: Jack Antonoff fronts The Bleachers, an indie pop band. His associated acts list reads like a who's who of emotionally complicated collaborations: Lorde, St. Vincent, Grimes, Chvrches, Carly Rae Jepsen.
details: Associated: Lorde, St. Vincent, Grimes, Chvrches
Vibe: arts kid from NJ who got too successful

edges.label: co-wrote 4 songs
edges.description: Jack co-wrote 4 songs on Taylor's 1989. This predates Melodrama by a year and established his place in the pop universe.
details: Significance: opened doors to Lorde's world

edges.label: wrote & released
edges.description: Lorde wrote and released Melodrama in 2017. Took 18 months. Is about a house party. Sure.
details: Official theme: solitude, a single house party
Real theme: see every other node

edges.label: co-wrote & produced
edges.description: Jack co-wrote and produced almost every track on Melodrama. With Antonoff credited on all but one song, his fingerprints were more obvious than Mastermotivation, and notably Reputation — perhaps reflecting the intense bond of which it was born.
details: Credit: on all but one song
Location: his home studio in Brooklyn
Concurrent: also producing Taylor Swift's Reputation

edges.label: official inspiration
edges.description: The official story. Lorde broke up with James in 2015 and wrote a breakup album. Green Light is about moving on from him. This is what the Wikipedia article says. The author does not believe it for one second.
details: Official narrative: breakup album about James
Wikipedia version: concept album about solitude after breakup
Reality check: 18 months in Jack's apartment

edges.label: unwitting muse
edges.description: Lena lent her apartment as recording studio, her emotional sustenance, and her relationship as the dramatic backdrop for an entire album. "Ella & Jack worked on most of this album in our apartment," Lena wrote on Instagram. Post since deleted.
details: Contribution: apartment, emotional support, dramatic irony
Awareness: did not know the full picture
"Lena's not really an award-winning cook" — Lorde

edges.label: debut album
edges.description: Lorde's debut, released 2013, when she was 16. Before Jack, before Melodrama, before all of this. A simpler time.

edges.label: track 1
edges.description: Green Light is the opening track and lead single from Melodrama. The album's "first chapter."

edges.label: track 3
edges.description: Sober is track 3 on Melodrama.

edges.label: track 4
edges.description: The Louvre is track 4 on Melodrama.

edges.label: track 5
edges.description: Liability is track 5 on Melodrama.

edges.label: track 6
edges.description: Hard Feelings/Loveless is track 6 on Melodrama.

edges.label: track 8
edges.description: Writer in the Dark is track 8 on Melodrama.

edges.label: track 9
edges.description: Sober II (Melodrama) is track 9 on Melodrama.

edges.label: references
edges.description: Green Light references the apartment (Lena's), the summer being over, Lena returning from campaigning. "I do my makeup in somebody else's car / we order different drinks at the same bar." Being sneaky so no one sees them together.
details: "I do my makeup in somebody else's car"
"We order different drinks at the same bar"
"Bein real sneaky so no one sees us together"
Summer 16: Lena is gone, Lena comes back

edges.label: suspected about
edges.description: Sober is "inspired by lord & jack's coachella trip??? Like LOL" per the author. "But my hips have missed your hips." Honestly what else do you need.
details: "But my hips have missed your hips"

edges.label: suspected about
edges.description: The Louvre is about an obsessive, consuming love — suspected to be about Jack. "We banged all summer." The summer is Lena's summer away. "But now she's back and jack is gonna pay."
details: "But now she's back and jack is gonna pay"

edges.label: written next to him
edges.description: Literally written in secret next to Jack while Lena slept. Lorde: "I woke up in the middle of the night and was lying next to someone... I wrote it down on my phone... While somebody's sleeping, like an evil witch." I need a moment.
details: Written: in secret, next to Jack, while Lena slept
Key lyric: "Bet you rue the day you kissed a writer in the dark"
Context: summer 16 ending, Jack not leaving Lena
Author reaction: I need a moment

edges.label: Jack and Jill
edges.description: "Jack and Jill got fucked up and possessive." The lyric is literally in the song. Also: "Baby really hurt me, crying to the bar." Lena comes back, Jack gets cold and possessive. This is not subtle.
details: "Jack and Jill got fucked up and possessive"
"Baby really hurt me, crying to the bar"

edges.label: suspected about
edges.description: The complicated, bitter end of whatever the Lorde/Jack situation was. "Bet you wanna rip my heart out." After this comes Loveless, which is... a lot.
details: "Bet you wanna rip my heart out"
Suspected: the end of their situation
And then we get loveless. which is. a lot.

edges.label: references
edges.description: Written April 2016, around when Lena finished the campaign. Lorde had "conflicted feelings" about the song. The song where she wonders what they do when they're sober — i.e., when Lena is back and they can't keep pretending.
details: Lena: back from 18-month campaign around this time
Lorde: conflicted feelings about the song
Theme: what happens when pretending is over

edges.label: first meeting?
edges.description: One account: Lorde and Jack first met at a Grammy after-party in 2014, introduced by Taylor Swift. Taylor got her a can of pineapple juice. Jack struck her as appealingly intense.

edges.label: first meeting?
edges.description: Jack may have met Lorde at this Grammy party. The pineapple juice was his doing. The rest is history.

edges.label: the introducer
edges.description: Taylor Swift reportedly introduced Lorde and Jack at this party. She created the monster. She has since been demoted from Lorde's friend group.
details: Taylor's luck: she's fine, she kept Lena

edges.label: attended
edges.description: Lorde attended Coachella 2016 with Jack. Had a major epiphany about Melodrama here. Sober was the result.
details: Epiphany: what the album was supposed to be

edges.label: attended
edges.description: Jack attended Coachella with Lorde in April 2016. Lena was presumably not there. The audacity.

edges.label: inspired
edges.description: Sober was directly inspired by Lorde and Jack's Coachella trip. The author notes this with a ???. Like LOL.

edges.label: performed
edges.description: Lorde performed Green Light and Liability on SNL in March 2017 wearing a wedding dress. Nobody asked questions. Everyone should have asked questions.
details: Author: I wasn't fucking joking about the wedding dress

edges.label: accompanied on piano
edges.description: Jack accompanied Lorde on piano during her SNL performance. They leaned back to back. Lena presumably watched this on TV. I will never get over this.
details: Staging: back to back with Lorde
Author: I will never get over this
